Overview

Address
46 MT EBO ROAD NORTH, BREWSTER, NY 10509
Phone
8452783636
Certified beds
160
Avg daily residents
146 (91% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit LLC
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
2000-08-29
Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.52 4.03 3.37 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.59 0.71 0.56 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.06 0.90 1.01 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.88 2.43 1.80 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.64 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.12 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.04
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.33
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.91
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
47.6%
annual
RN turnover
20.0%
annual
Administrators departed
2
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.43
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.04
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2023-10-25 25 11 21 144 1 144
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2020-09-23 11 1 10 64 1 64
Total weighted health score
124.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (44)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

43 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0561 Honor the resident's right to and the facility must promote and facilitate resident self-determination through support of resident choice. D 2025-09-29 2025-11-21
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-09-29 2025-11-21
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2025-09-29 2025-11-21
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. D 2025-09-29 2025-11-21
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2025-09-29 2025-11-21
0741 Ensure that the facility has sufficient staff members who possess the competencies and skills to meet the behavioral health needs of residents. D 2025-09-29 2025-11-21
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2025-07-07 2025-08-29
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2025-04-23 2025-07-07
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2025-04-23 2025-07-07
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-04-23 2025-07-07
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. E 2025-04-23 2025-07-07
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2025-04-23 2025-07-07
0811 Ensure that residents are assessed for appropriateness for a feeding assistant program, receive services as per their plan of care, and feeding assistants are trained and supervised. D 2025-04-23 2025-07-07
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-04-23 2025-07-07
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. E 2024-12-16 2025-01-29
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-12-16 2025-01-29
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2024-12-16 2025-01-29
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. E 2024-12-16 2025-01-29
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. E 2024-12-16 2025-01-29
0838 Conduct and document a facility-wide assessment to determine what resources are necessary to care for residents competently during both day-to-day operations (including nights and weekends) and emergencies. D 2024-12-16 2025-01-29
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2023-10-25 2023-12-22
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2023-10-25 2023-12-22
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2023-10-25 2023-12-22
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2023-10-25 2023-12-22
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2023-10-25 2023-12-22
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2023-10-25 2023-12-22
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2023-10-25 2023-12-22
0728 Ensure that nurse aides who have worked more than 4 months, are trained and competent; and nurse aides who have worked less than 4 months are enrolled in appropriate training. E 2023-10-25 2023-12-22
0730 Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. E 2023-10-25 2023-12-22
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2023-10-25 2023-12-22
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2023-10-25 2023-12-22
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2023-10-25 2023-12-22
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2023-10-25 2023-12-22
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2023-08-14 2023-10-12
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. E 2023-08-14 2023-10-12
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. E 2020-09-23 2020-11-16
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2018-07-25 2018-09-24
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2018-07-25 2018-09-24
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2018-07-25 2018-09-24
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2018-07-25 2018-09-24
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2018-07-25 2018-09-24
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. D 2018-07-25 2018-09-24
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2018-07-25 2018-09-24
0947 Ensure nurse aides have the skills they need to care for residents, and give nurse aides education in dementia care and abuse prevention. E 2018-07-25 2018-09-24

Deficiencies 0811 — description Ensure that residents are assessed for appropriateness for a feeding assistant program, receive services as per their plan of care, and feeding assistants are trained and supervised. 0811.description
